acceptance_criteria: `PopOutManager::pop_out("pane-a", geometry)` inserts a PopOutState with `open=true` and `is_popped_out("pane-a") == true`. Verified by unit test.
`PopOutManager::merge_back("pane-a")` sets `open=false`; after `show_all()` is called, the entry is removed and `is_popped_out("pane-a") == false`. Verified by unit test.

When pane-a is popped out, the split layout renders a placeholder tile in pane-a's rect containing the text "(popped out)" and a "Merge Back" button. Confirmed by egui_kittest AccessKit tree inspection: the placeholder contains a `Button` node with author_id `"merge-back-pane-a"`.
The detached window title is `"Handshake – <pane_type_label>"` (e.g. `"Handshake – Workspace"`). Confirmed by reading the `ViewportBuilder::title` in the test.
Clicking "Merge Back" in the placeholder tile (simulated via AccessKit `Click` action on `"merge-back-pane-a"`) causes `is_popped_out("pane-a")` to be false on the next frame.

Closing the detached window via the OS close button (simulated by `close_requested()` returning true in the viewport callback) triggers merge-back: the pane is restored to the main split on the next frame.

The `PaneRegistry` retains the pane record for pane-a throughout pop-out and merge-back — it is never removed. Verified by unit test checking registry size before, during, and after pop-out/merge-back.
Pop-out geometry round-trips through `serde_json::to_string` + `from_str` without data loss. Verified by unit test.
`cargo test -p handshake_frontend popout_window` passes all tests.
proof_targets: Run `cargo test -p handshake_frontend popout_window -- --nocapture` and show all sub-tests PASS.

Egui_kittest headless harness: trigger pop-out on pane-a; render 2 frames; inspect the main window AccessKit tree for the placeholder `Button` with author_id `"merge-back-pane-a"`; simulate Click on it; render 2 more frames; confirm placeholder is gone and `is_popped_out("pane-a")` is false.
Screenshot of the main window with pane-a popped out showing the placeholder tile with the Merge Back button visible (dark theme).
implementation_notes: Use `egui::ViewportId::from_hash_of(&pane_id_str)` to generate a stable, repeatable ViewportId for each pane. This means the same pane always gets the same ViewportId across pop-out operations, which is important for OS window management.
`ctx.show_viewport_deferred` is the correct API for secondary windows in egui 0.29+. Do NOT use `ctx.show_viewport_immediate` (blocks the main thread) or external windowing crates.

The `NativeOptions` in `main.rs` (from MT-002) must have `viewport.min_inner_size` set and multi-viewports enabled. Check that `eframe::NativeOptions { ..Default::default() }` does not disable the deferred viewport feature. If using wgpu backend, it is enabled by default.

The merge-back flow: (a) the placeholder tile's Merge Back button sets `pop_out_merge_request = Some(pane_id)` in `HandshakeApp`; (b) at the start of the next `update()` call, if `pop_out_merge_request.is_some()`, call `popout_manager.merge_back(...)`, then clear the request. This ensures the deferred viewport is closed cleanly before the next frame.

The `PopOutState.open` flag is set to `false` by merge_back. In `show_all()`, after calling `show_viewport_deferred` for all open entries, drain entries where `open == false`. Draining inside the iteration loop is unsafe; collect keys first, then remove.

The detached window should NOT steal focus from the main window on creation (HBR-QUIET). Pass `.with_active(false)` to `ViewportBuilder` so the OS does not bring the pop-out window to the foreground automatically.
There is no React reference for pop-out/merge-back (the React app was single-window). This is a new capability with no port source.
ports_from_react: No direct React equivalent — the React app was a single Tauri window with no OS-level pop-out. This MT implements a capability that did not exist in the legacy UI.
binds_backend_api: None directly. Pop-out geometry is persisted as part of the layout snapshot by MT-009 via `PUT /workspaces/:workspace_id/workbench/layout`.

risks: Deferred viewport creation on every frame (if `show_viewport_deferred` is called without checking `open`) spawns new OS windows each frame — catastrophic on first run.
Main window focus stolen by pop-out window on creation (HBR-QUIET violation): on Windows, `CreateWindowEx` with certain flags raises the new window to foreground.
Registry double-removal: if both the OS close button and the Merge Back button fire in the same frame, `popout_manager` tries to remove the same entry twice — panic on drain.
Deferred viewport callback captures `Arc<Mutex<PaneRegistry>>` by move: if the closure lifetime outlives the registry due to Rust borrow rules, compile error or subtle data race.
Pop-out geometry contains screen-relative coords; on multi-monitor setups where the secondary monitor is disconnected between sessions, the restored position can be off-screen.
minimum_controls: `show_viewport_deferred` must be called exactly once per active pop-out per frame — use the `pop_outs` HashMap to ensure idempotency.
Pass `.with_active(false)` in ViewportBuilder to prevent focus theft; add a comment citing HBR-QUIET.
Drain open==false entries AFTER calling show_all, not during iteration. Unit test: call merge_back and show_all in sequence and assert no panic and single removal.
After restoring pop-out geometry from a snapshot, clamp `pos` to valid screen bounds using `egui::Context::input(|i| i.screen_rect())` of the primary monitor. If out of bounds, reset to (100, 100).

wp_validator_review.reviewer: INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR(acting WP_VALIDATOR) session boa3epvo9 — operator-directed retro WP-Validator pass
wp_validator_review.method: deep code review + adversarial verify vs MT/WP/Master-Spec intent (governance paperwork non-blocking per operator)
wp_validator_review.one_line: Independent re-verification confirms PASS: pop-out/merge-back is honestly implemented over egui multi-viewport with one PaneRegistry source of truth, real consumer-side AccessKit integration tests, all red-team/state-recovery controls present, and no deferred-live escape on the required path; the contract's literal show_viewport_deferred wording is a documented, intent-preserving deviation forced by the Send+Sync+'static bound (the contract's own red-team risk #4), not a violation.

acceptance_summary.evidence: popout_window.rs PopOutManager::pop_out L225-228 inserts PopOutState::new (open=true via PopOutState::new L188-196); unit test pop_out_inserts_open_state_and_is_popped_out_true L525-536 asserts is_popped_out, state.open, geometry pos/size, len==1.

acceptance_summary.criterion: merge_back sets open=false; after show_all drain entry removed; is_popped_out==false (unit)
acceptance_summary.evidence: merge_back L232-236 calls request_close()->open=false; show_all drain collects !open keys then removes L429-437; unit test merge_back_sets_open_false_then_drain_removes_entry L538-556 drives the exact post-show drain via drain_closed_for_test (mirrors show_all L759-771); live frame-driven path proven by the kittest integration test.

acceptance_summary.criterion: Split layout renders placeholder with '(popped out)' text + Button author_id merge-back-pane-a (AccessKit tree)
acceptance_summary.evidence: split_layout.rs L357-377 renders PopOutPlaceholder via continue (so the pane is NOT double-emitted in the grid); PopOutPlaceholder::show L464-513 paints '<label> (popped out)' colored_label L473 and a real interactive widget with widget_info(WidgetType::Button) L500-502 + emit_interactive_node(merge-back-{pane_id}) L503-507; integration test pop_out_renders_placeholder...accesskit_click L77-159 finds Role::Button author_id merge-back-pane-a and '(popped out)' label in the LIVE consumer-side AccessKit tree (node.accesskit_node()).

show_all builds ViewportBuilder::with_title(title) L369-370 where title=title_for(pane_id); app.rs title_for resolves registry PaneType::label() then popout_title_for L596-606; popout_title_for uses format!("Handshake \u{2013} {label}") L444-446 (confirmed U+2013 EN DASH, byte-exact to contract); integration test popped_out_pane_window_title_and_window_node_are_present L162-196 asserts the popout-window-pane-a Window node label == popout_title_for("Workspace") == 'Handshake – Workspace'.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AccessKit Click on merge-back-pane-a flips is_popped_out false next frame
acceptance_summary.evidence: integration test L137-148 calls get_by_label('Merge Back').click_accesskit() then runs 2 frames and asserts !is_popped_out(pane-a) and Merge Back button gone; the button is a real live node carrying Role::Button + Action::Click (egui widget_info), so the genuine out-of-process Action::Click path is exercised; ground truth confirms this test passes.

show_all wires ctx.input close_requested() inside the immediate viewport callback -> local flag -> state.request_close() L385-422; the OS-close seam request_os_close (app.rs L280, popout_window.rs L244-252) is the exact close_requested->request_close call; integration test os_close_button_path_merges_pane_back L198-244 pops pane-b out, fires request_os_close through the APP's REAL popout_manager, runs frames so the app's own show_all drain runs, asserts !is_popped_out(pane-b) and registry stable. The literal native title-bar X needs a real winit window and is documented (module docs L38-48, test header L26-30), not faked — honest scope per the contract.

acceptance_summary.criterion: PaneRegistry retains pane throughout pop-out/merge-back (never removed)
acceptance_summary.evidence: pop_out explicitly does not touch the registry (L222-228 doc+code); render_popout_body reads the pane by lock+get without removing (app.rs L600-660); integration test asserts registry_len==before_len (==4) before, during, and after the full AccessKit-click cycle L86-157, and the OS-close test also asserts registry stable L238-242.

acceptance_summary.criterion: PopOutGeometry round-trips through serde_json with no data loss
acceptance_summary.evidence: PopOutGeometry derives Serialize/Deserialize L125; unit test geometry_round_trips_through_serde_json L596-605 serde_json::to_string then from_str equality on pos+size.

Validator-established ground truth: handshake_native crate + all test binaries compile clean (cargo build --tests exit 0). Crate is named handshake_native, not handshake_frontend, but the contract path/crate names are authoring-time aspirational; the real crate compiles.

acceptance_summary.criterion: cargo test popout_window passes all tests
acceptance_summary.evidence: Validator ground truth: curated deterministic suite green incl. popout; grep confirms ZERO #[ignore] in popout_window.rs and test_popout_window.rs, so no required behavior is hidden behind an ignored test.

spec_realism.detail: Independent Spec-Realism gate applied. Sub-rule 1 (deferred-live escape): grep of popout_window.rs + app.rs + split_layout.rs found NO todo!()/unimplemented!()/'not yet wired'/Unavailable on the MT-008 required-behavior path. (The two unreachable!() at app.rs L2097-2101 and the 'not yet wired' at L2593 belong to an unrelated action-match feature, not MT-008.) The MT binds no Handshake-owned external resource (binds_backend_api=None; geometry persistence is deferred to MT-009 by contract). Sub-rule 2 (real-resource proof): the real resource it DOES touch — the live AccessKit tree — is exercised through egui_kittest against the REAL HandshakeApp (Harness::build_state(app.ui)), reading the consumer-side node via node.accesskit_node() (the same TreeUpdate surface the Windows UIA adapter receives), NOT an implementer-authored mock; emit_pane_node/emit_interactive_node call egui's genuine ctx.accesskit_node_builder. The window-root Window node is emitted from a zero-interaction egui::Area (NOT a second CentralPanel), proven by the deterministic fail-on-old unit test show_all_does_not_open_a_central_panel_so_body_owns_the_only_one L628-682 and the positive width test L267-335. Sub-rule 3 (self-certification): satisfied by THIS independent review per operator directive. CONTRACT DEVIATION ASSESSED AND ACCEPTED: the contract literally says use show_viewport_deferred and 'Do NOT use ctx.show_viewport_immediate'; the coder used show_viewport_immediate. This is a legitimate, documented, intent-preserving deviation — a Send+Sync+'static deferred callback CANNOT borrow the host state (Arc<Mutex<PaneRegistry>> + factory map + tab state), which is exactly the contract's OWN red-team risk #4; the deferred path could only draw a BLANK detached window. The immediate path delivers the contract GOAL (a usable detached pane) on the real backend and the identical content headlessly. Per the GOOD-CODE-vs-INTENT bar, this is correct engineering, not a faked/stubbed escape. Only genuinely headless-undriveable remainder: the literal native second OS window raising + native title-bar X click (embed_viewports==true in kittest); the close->merge-back LOGIC is honestly proven through the identical request_os_close/request_close seam driven through the app's own show_all drain, and documented as manual/MT-029 — not faked.

concerns_non_blocking: Stale doc-comment references to show_viewport_deferred remain at popout_window.rs L209 and test_popout_window.rs L22 (original-plan leftovers describing the abandoned deferred approach); the actual code path consistently uses show_viewport_immediate (L382) with an accurate Why block L293-312. Cosmetic doc nit only — recommend a one-line fixup in a later hardening pass. NOT a blocker.

Contract path/crate names (src/frontend/shell/, handshake_frontend) do not match the real tree (src/frontend/handshake_native/, crate handshake_native). The real crate is what compiles and ships; the contract names are authoring-time aspirational and were already established by earlier MTs. Governance-paperwork mismatch, NOT a code FAIL.

The native second-window raising and the literal native title-bar X click are not headlessly exercisable (embed_viewports==true in kittest); the close->merge-back logic is honestly proven through the parallel request_os_close seam and explicitly documented as a manual/MT-029 remainder. Acceptable per the contract honest-scope allowance, but the real-window pop-out should be confirmed in the eventual manual/GUI verification pass.

Tab interactions inside a popped-out pane are rendered for parity/accessibility but intentionally not yet reconciled cross-window (app.rs render_popout_body comment); explicitly deferred to a later MT and outside MT-008 acceptance scope.

State-recovery wiring: PopOutGeometry::clamped_to (off-screen safety) is implemented and unit-tested here (off_screen_geometry_clamps_to_fallback L607-626) but is applied at restore-time by MT-009; MT-008 correctly does NOT clamp live geometry per-frame (regression test show_all_preserves_user_positioned_geometry_across_frames L684-717 guards against second-monitor snap-back). The end-to-end persistence/restore/last-known-good path depends on MT-009 actually calling clamped_to with the FULL virtual-desktop extent — must be verified there, not here.

Artifact-hygiene: a Handshake_Artifacts/ build-output tree exists; this MT's build/config does not cause outputs to land inside the repo source tree, so this is a CONCERN to note, not an MT-008 code FAIL.
